pattern: Resolve every replacement against the original string and apply them in left-to-right index order so that earlier edits never shift the positions the later ones were measured against.
hints: Each operation is checked against the original string, not against the partially-edited result, so decide first which operations actually fire.
If you walk the string from left to right and process the operations sorted by their start index, the pieces you keep and the pieces you replace simply concatenate in order.

Sort the operations by index. Scan the original string with a cursor; at each start index whose source matches there, append the target and jump the cursor past the source, otherwise copy the current character. The non-overlap guarantee makes this unambiguous.

edge_inputs.description: both operations fire and rewrite disjoint regions
second source does not match at its index so it is skipped
operations supplied out of index order must still apply correctly
source longer than remaining suffix cannot match
target shorter than source shrinks the string
no operation matches leaves the string untouched

stress.description: long random string with many non-overlapping single-character operations, some matching and some not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    idxs = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), min(n, 100)))
    indices, sources, targets = [], [], []
    for i in idxs:
        # half the time use the real char (will match), half a wrong char
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            src = s[i]
        else:
            src = rng.choice('xyz')
        indices.append(i)
        sources.append(src)
        targets.append(rng.choice(['Q', 'WW', 'EEE']))
    return [s, indices, sources, targets]
stress.note: single-char sources guarantee non-overlap regardless of which fire
